title: William Henry Perkin
content_html:
William Henry Perkin

The man who invented synthetic dyes

William Henry Perkin was born on March 12, 1838, in London. As a boy, Perkin’s curiosity prompted early interests in the arts, sciences, photography, and engineering. But it was a chance stumbling upon a run-down, yet functional, laboratory in his late grandfather’s home that solidified the young man’s enthusiasm for chemistry.

As a student at the City of London School, Perkin became immersed in the study of chemistry. His talent and devotion to the subject were perceived by his teacher, Thomas Hall, who encouraged him to attend a series of lectures given by the eminent scientist Michael Faraday at the Royal Institution. Those speeches fired the young chemist’s enthusiasm further, and he later went on to attend the Royal College of Chemistry, which he succeeded in entering in 1853, at the age of 15.

At the time of Perkin’s enrollment, the Royal College of Chemistry was headed by the noted German chemist August Wilhelm Hofmann. Perkin’s scientific gifts soon caught Hofmann’s attention and within two years, he became Hofmann’s youngest assistant. Not long after that, Perkin made the scientific breakthrough that would bring him both fame and fortune.

At the time, quinine was the only viable medical treatment for malaria. The drug is derived from the bark of the cinchona tree, native to South America and by 1856 demand for the drug was surpassing the available supply. Thus, when Hofmann made some passing comments about the desirability of a synthetic substitute for quinine, it was unsurprising that his star pupil was moved to take up the challenge.

During his vacation in 1856, Perkin spent his time in the laboratory on the top floor of his family’s house. He was attempting to manufacture quinine from aniline, an inexpensive and readily available coal tar waste product. Despite his best efforts, however, he did not end up with quinine. Instead, he produced a mysterious dark sludge. Luckily, Perkin’s scientific training and nature prompted him to investigate the substance further. Incorporating potassium dichromate and alcohol into the aniline at various stages of the experimental process, he finally produced a deep purple solution. And, proving the truth of the famous scientist Louis Pasteur’s words “chance favors only the prepared mind”, Perkin saw the potential of his unexpected find.

Historically, textile dyes were made from such natural sources as plants and animal excretions. Some of these, such as the glandular mucus of snails, were difficult to obtain and outrageously expensive. Indeed, the purple colour extracted from a snail was once so costly that in society at the time only the rich could afford it. Further, natural dyes tended to be muddy in hue and fade quickly. It was against this backdrop that Perkin’s discovery was made.

Perkin quickly grasped that his purple solution could be used to colour fabric, thus making it the world’s first synthetic dye. Realising the importance of this breakthrough, he lost no time in patenting it. But perhaps the most fascinating of all Perkin’s reactions to his find was his nearly instant recognition that the new dye had commercial possibilities.

Perkin originally named his dye Tyrian Purple, but it later became commonly known as mauve (from the French for the plant used to make the colour violet). He asked advice of Scottish dye works owner Robert Pullar, who assured him that manufacturing the dye would be well worth it if the colour remained fast (i.e. would not fade) and the cost was relatively low. So, over the fierce objections of his mentor Hofmann, he left college to give birth to the modern chemical industry.

With the help of his father and brother, Perkin set up a factory not far from London. Utilizing the cheap and plentiful coal tar that was an almost unlimited byproduct of London’s gas street lighting, the dye works began producing the world’s first synthetically dyed material in 1857. The company received a commercial boost from Eugénie de Montijo of France, when she decided the new color flattered her. Very soon, mauve was the necessary shade for all the fashionable ladies in that country. Not to be outdone, Queen Victoria also appeared in public wearing a mauve gown, thus making it all the rage in England as well. The dye was bold and fast, and the public clamoured for more. Perkin went back to the drawing board.

Although Perkin’s fame was achieved and fortune assured by his first discovery, the chemist continued his research. Among other dyes he developed and introduced were aniline red (1859) and aniline black (1863) and in the late 1860s, Perkin’s green. It is important to note that Perkin’s synthetic dye discoveries had outcomes far beyond the merely decorative. The dyes also became vital to medical research in many ways. For instance, they were used to stain previously invisible microbes and bacteria, allowing researchers to identify such bacilli as tuberculosis, cholera, and anthrax. Artificial dyes continue to play a crucial role today. And, in what would have been particularly pleasing to Perkin, their current use is in the search for a vaccine against malaria.

questions.groupHtml:
Questions 1–6

Complete the sentences below.

Choose ONE WORD AND/OR A NUMBER from the passage for each answer.
questions.group_html:
Questions 1–7

Do the following statements agree with the information given in the Reading Passage?

In boxes 1–7 on your answer sheet, write

  • TRUE if the statement agrees with the information
  • FALSE if the statement contradicts the information
  • NOT GIVEN if there is no information on this
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Xác định keyword trong câu hỏi

Michael Faraday
first person
recognise Perkin’s ability
chemistry

Bước 2: Scan bài đọc tìm “Michael Faraday”

Đoạn 2 có:

“His talent and devotion to the subject were perceived by his teacher, Thomas Hall…”

“perceived” = nhận ra.

Bước 3: So sánh với câu hỏi

Người đầu tiên nhận ra năng lực của Perkin là Thomas Hall, không phải Michael Faraday.

=> FALSE.

_blockConfig.noteContent:
**A type of parrot:**
- diet consists of fern fronds, various parts of a tree and [1]
- nests are created in [2] where eggs are laid.
**Arrival of settlers**
- the [3] of the kākāpō were used to make clothes.

questions.questionHtml:
• sentence with blank 1_______

• another sentence with 2_______
questions.question_text: Michael Faraday was the first person to recognise Perkin's ability as a student of chemistry.

questions.explanation:
NOT GIVEN

Bước 1: Keyword

Michael Faraday
suggested
enroll in the Royal College of Chemistry

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn chứa Faraday

Đoạn nói:

“Thomas Hall... encouraged him to attend a series of lectures given by Michael Faraday…”

Sau đó mới nói Perkin học Royal College of Chemistry.

Bước 3: Kiểm tra thông tin

Bài không hề nói Faraday khuyên Perkin vào trường này.

IELTS rất hay bẫy kiểu:

có nhắc đến người đó
có nhắc đến trường đó
nhưng không nói hai thứ liên quan với nhau.

=> NOT GIVEN.
questions.question_text: Michael Faraday suggested Perkin should enrol in the Royal College of Chemistry.

questions.explanation:
FALSE

Bước 1: Keyword

employed
Hofmann
assistant

Bước 2: Scan “assistant”

Đoạn 3:

“he became Hofmann’s youngest assistant.”

Bước 3: Phân tích

Cấu trúc:

Perkin = assistant
Hofmann = boss

Đề đảo ngược:
“Perkin employed Hofmann”

=> hoàn toàn trái nghĩa.

=> FALSE.
questions.question_text: Perkin employed August Wilhelm Hofmann as his assistant.

questions.explanation:
TRUE

Bước 1: Keyword

still young
discovery
rich and famous

Bước 2: Tìm thông tin tuổi tác

Đoạn trước nói:

“...entering in 1853, at the age of 15.”

Sau đó:

“Not long after that, Perkin made the scientific breakthrough…”

Bước 3: Suy luận

“Not long after” = không lâu sau đó.
Nghĩa là ông vẫn còn trẻ khi phát minh ra thuốc nhuộm nổi tiếng.

=> TRUE.
questions.question_text: Perkin was still young when he made the discovery that made him rich and famous.

questions.explanation:
NOT GIVEN

Bước 1: Keyword

trees
quinine
only in South America

Bước 2: Tìm “South America”

Đoạn 4:

“the cinchona tree, native to South America”

Bước 3: Phân tích bẫy

“native to” = có nguồn gốc từ.
KHÔNG đồng nghĩa với:
“grow only in” = chỉ mọc ở.

Bài không nói cây này chỉ tồn tại ở Nam Mỹ.

=> NOT GIVEN.
questions.question_text: The trees from which quinine is derived grow only in South America.

questions.explanation:
TRUE

Bước 1: Keyword

manufacture a drug
coal tar waste product

Bước 2: Scan “coal tar”

Đoạn 5:

“He was attempting to manufacture quinine from aniline, an inexpensive and readily available coal tar waste product.”

Bước 3: Phân tích

quinine = thuốc trị malaria
aniline = sản phẩm thải từ coal tar

Perkin muốn tạo thuốc từ chất thải coal tar.

=> TRUE.
questions.question_text: Perkin hoped to manufacture a drug from a coal tar waste product.

questions.explanation:
NOT GIVEN

Bước 1: Keyword

inspired
Louis Pasteur

Bước 2: Tìm “Louis Pasteur”

Đoạn có:

“chance favors only the prepared mind”

Đây chỉ là câu trích dẫn.

Bước 3: Kiểm tra

Bài không nói Perkin được truyền cảm hứng từ Pasteur hay nghiên cứu của ông.

=> NOT GIVEN.
questions.question_text: Perkin was inspired by the discoveries of the famous scientist Louis Pasteur.

questions.group_html:
Questions 8–13

Answer the questions below.

Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer.

Write your answers in boxes 8–13 on your answer sheet.
questions.explanation:
the rich

Bước 1: Keyword

purple
associated with society group

Bước 2: Scan “purple”

Đoạn 6:

“only the rich could afford it.”

Bước 3: Lấy đúng từ trong bài

=> the rich

Lưu ý:
Không viết “rich people” vì đề yêu cầu NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS, nhưng phải lấy đúng wording trong passage sẽ an toàn hơn.
questions.question_text: Before Perkin's discovery, with what group in society was the colour purple associated?

questions.explanation:
commercial possibilities

Bước 1: Keyword

immediately understand
potential

Bước 2: Scan “potential”

Đoạn 7:

“the new dye had commercial possibilities.”

Bước 3: Chép đúng cụm từ

=> commercial possibilities
questions.question_text: What potential did Perkin immediately understand that his new dye had?

questions.explanation:
mauve

Bước 1: Keyword

finally used
first colour

Bước 2: Tìm tên màu

Đoạn 8:

“it later became commonly known as mauve”

Ban đầu tên là Tyrian Purple, nhưng tên cuối cùng được dùng phổ biến là “mauve”.

=> mauve
questions.question_text: What was the name finally used to refer to the first colour Perkin invented?

questions.explanation:
Robert Pullar

Bước 1: Keyword

consulted
before setting up

Bước 2: Scan tên riêng

Đoạn 8:

“He asked advice of Scottish dye works owner Robert Pullar…”

Bước 3: Chép đúng tên

=> Robert Pullar
questions.question_text: What was the name of the person Perkin consulted before setting up his own dye works?

questions.explanation:
France

Bước 1: Keyword

first become fashionable

Bước 2: Scan đoạn nói về thời trang

Đoạn 9:

“The company received a commercial boost from the Empress Eugenio of France…”

và:

“Very soon, mauve was the necessary shade for all the fashionable ladies in that country.”

“that country” = France.

Sau đó mới nói Queen Victoria làm màu này nổi ở Anh.
questions.question_text: In what country did Perkin's newly invented colour first become fashionable?

questions.explanation:
malaria

Bước 1: Keyword

disease
researchers
synthetic dyes

Bước 2: Scan đoạn cuối

“their current use is in the search for a vaccine against malaria.”

Bước 3: Lấy đúng từ

=> malaria
questions.question_text: According to the passage, which disease is now being targeted by researchers using synthetic dyes?
